verses.text: 'O LORD, thou hast searched me, and known me,' my sitting and rising, my thoughts from afar. God knows him utterly. There is no corner of our life or mind hidden from the all-seeing God.
'O LORD, thou hast searched me, and known me,' my sitting and rising, my thoughts from afar. God knows him utterly. There is no corner of our life or mind hidden from the all-seeing God.
'O LORD, thou hast searched me, and known me,' my sitting and rising, my thoughts from afar. God knows him utterly. There is no corner of our life or mind hidden from the all-seeing God.

Before a word is on his tongue, God knows it altogether, hemming him in behind and before; 'such knowledge is too wonderful for me.' God's knowledge is total and amazing. The mind reels before a God who knows us better than we know ourselves.

Before a word is on his tongue, God knows it altogether, hemming him in behind and before; 'such knowledge is too wonderful for me.' God's knowledge is total and amazing. The mind reels before a God who knows us better than we know ourselves.

Before a word is on his tongue, God knows it altogether, hemming him in behind and before; 'such knowledge is too wonderful for me.' God's knowledge is total and amazing. The mind reels before a God who knows us better than we know ourselves.

Where could he flee from God's Spirit? In heaven, in the grave, or the farthest sea, 'thy hand shall lead me.' God is everywhere. There is no place beyond His presence, and for the believer that presence is a guiding hand.

Where could he flee from God's Spirit? In heaven, in the grave, or the farthest sea, 'thy hand shall lead me.' God is everywhere. There is no place beyond His presence, and for the believer that presence is a guiding hand.

Where could he flee from God's Spirit? In heaven, in the grave, or the farthest sea, 'thy hand shall lead me.' God is everywhere. There is no place beyond His presence, and for the believer that presence is a guiding hand.

Where could he flee from God's Spirit? In heaven, in the grave, or the farthest sea, 'thy hand shall lead me.' God is everywhere. There is no place beyond His presence, and for the believer that presence is a guiding hand.
Even darkness cannot hide him, for 'the darkness and the light are both alike to thee.' Night is as day to God. No shadow can conceal us from Him, nor any secret escape His sight.
Even darkness cannot hide him, for 'the darkness and the light are both alike to thee.' Night is as day to God. No shadow can conceal us from Him, nor any secret escape His sight.

God formed his inward parts and wove him in the womb; 'I am fearfully and wonderfully made,' his frame and days written in God's book. We are God's intricate handiwork. The wonder of our making testifies to the wisdom and care of our Maker.

God formed his inward parts and wove him in the womb; 'I am fearfully and wonderfully made,' his frame and days written in God's book. We are God's intricate handiwork. The wonder of our making testifies to the wisdom and care of our Maker.

God formed his inward parts and wove him in the womb; 'I am fearfully and wonderfully made,' his frame and days written in God's book. We are God's intricate handiwork. The wonder of our making testifies to the wisdom and care of our Maker.

God formed his inward parts and wove him in the womb; 'I am fearfully and wonderfully made,' his frame and days written in God's book. We are God's intricate handiwork. The wonder of our making testifies to the wisdom and care of our Maker.

'How precious also are thy thoughts unto me, O God!' more in number than the sand; 'when I awake, I am still with thee.' God's thoughts toward him are countless and dear. To wake each day in His presence is the believer's joy.

'How precious also are thy thoughts unto me, O God!' more in number than the sand; 'when I awake, I am still with thee.' God's thoughts toward him are countless and dear. To wake each day in His presence is the believer's joy.

He longs that God slay the wicked and parts from bloody men, hating those who hate God 'with perfect hatred.' His zeal is for God's honour. The believer's love for God breeds a holy hatred of all that opposes Him.

He longs that God slay the wicked and parts from bloody men, hating those who hate God 'with perfect hatred.' His zeal is for God's honour. The believer's love for God breeds a holy hatred of all that opposes Him.

He ends, 'Search me, O God, and know my heart... and lead me in the way everlasting.' He invites the searching God to cleanse him. The soul that knows God sees all asks Him to find and remove every secret sin.
